perturbation.original_text: 1.2 Definitions. The following terms shall have the meaning ascribed to them in this Section 1.2: A. "Affiliate" shall mean, with respect to a party, any entity at any time Controlling, Controlled by or under common Control with, such party. B. "Bank" shall mean each of the subsidiary financial institutions of Customer. C. "Change in Control" shall mean any event or series of events by which (i) any person or entity or group of persons or entities shall acquire Control of another person or entity or (ii) in the case of a corporation, during any period of 12 consecutive months commencing before or after the date hereof, individuals who at the beginning of such 12-month period were directors of such corporation shall cease for any reason to constitute a majority of the board of directors of such corporation. D. "Commencement Date" shall mean the date on which Conversion for all Banks has been completed. The parties anticipate the Commencement Date to be November 16, 1998. E. "Contract Year" shall mean a period commencing on the first day of the month in which the Commencement Date occurs (and each anniversary thereof) and terminating on the last date of the month occurring one (1) year thereafter. F. "Control" shall mean the direct or indirect ownership of over 50% of the capital stock (or other ownership interest, if not a corporation) of any entity or the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct the management and policies of such entity by ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise. "Controlling" shall mean having Control of any entity and "Controlled" shall mean being the subject of Control by another entity.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 Definitions. The following terms shall have the meaning ascribed to them in this Section 1.2: A. "Affiliate" shall mean, with respect to a party, any entity at any time affiliated or associated with, such party, including but not limited to subsidiary, parent, and sister company relationships. B. "Bank" shall mean each of the subsidiary financial institutions of Customer. C. "Change in Control" shall mean any event or series of events by which (i) any person or entity or group of persons or entities shall acquire Control of another person or entity or (ii) in the case of a corporation, during any period of 12 consecutive months commencing before or after the date hereof, individuals who at the beginning of such 12-month period were directors of such corporation shall cease for any reason to constitute a majority of the board of directors of such corporation. D. "Commencement Date" shall mean the date on which Conversion for all Banks has been completed. The parties anticipate the Commencement Date to be November 16, 1998. E. "Contract Year" shall mean a period commencing on the first day of the month in which the Commencement Date occurs (and each anniversary thereof) and terminating on the last date of the month occurring one (1) year thereafter. F. "Control" shall mean the direct or indirect ownership of over 50% of the capital stock (or other ownership interest, if not a corporation) of any entity or the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct the management and policies of such entity by ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise. "Controlling" shall mean having Control of any entity and "Controlled" shall mean being the subject of Control by another entity.
perturbation.explanation: This change to the definition of "Affiliate" in Section 1.2 creates an in-text contradiction. The original definition limited affiliates to entities under common control (ownership of over 50% of the capital stock), while the new definition broadens this to include any affiliated or associated entity, encompassing subsidiary, parent, and sister company relationships, regardless of ownership percentage. This contradicts the scope and intent of Section 21.10, which refers to the original definition to hold the Customer responsible for the actions of its affiliates and subsidiaries. Since affiliate definition is being broaden, it is unclear which companies are included, it creates uncertainty about Customer's responsibility over affiliate compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 21.10 Affiliates. All processing for Customer and Customer's subsidiaries and Affiliates which M&I does shall be included as part of the Services provided under this Agreement and shall be done in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement. Customer agrees that it is responsible for assuring compliance with the Agreement by its affiliates and subsidiaries. Customer agrees to be responsible for the submission of its affiliates' data to M&I for processing and for the transmission to Customer's affiliates of such data processed by and received from M&I. Customer agrees to pay any and all fees owed under this Agreement for Services rendered to it and its subsidiaries and other Affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The change to the definition of "Affiliate" broadens its scope significantly, from entities under common control (ownership of over 50% of the capital stock) to any affiliated or associated entity, including subsidiary, parent, and sister company relationships, regardless of ownership percentage. This directly contradicts Section 21.10, which holds the Customer responsible for the actions of its affiliates and subsidiaries, as the original definition of affiliate is what defines the scope of that responsibility, and now that the definition has been expanded, it creates an ambiguity in Section 21.10 as to who is accountable for what under the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 11. TERMINATION 11.1 For Convenience. Customer may terminate this Agreement during the Term upon at least one (1) years' written notice to M&I, provided that Customer pays M&I an early termination fee ("Termination for Convenience Fee") in an amount equal to REDACTED of the Estimated Remaining Value. The Termination for Convenience Fee shall apply to any early termination of this Agreement other than pursuant to an Event of Default on the part of Customer or M&I or pursuant to Section 11.3 below. Fifty percent of the Termination for Convenience Fee shall be paid to M&I within thirty (30) days following the date of Customer's notice and the remaining 50% shall be paid to M&I within thirty (30) days prior to the Effective Date of Termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. TERMINATION 11.1 For Convenience. Customer may terminate this Agreement during the Term upon at least one (1) years' written notice to M&I, provided that Customer pays M&I an early termination fee ("Termination for Convenience Fee") in an amount equal to REDACTED of the Estimated Remaining Value. The Termination for Convenience Fee shall apply to any early termination of this Agreement other than pursuant to an Event of Default on the part of Customer or M&I or pursuant to Section 11.3 below. One hundred percent of the Termination for Convenience Fee shall be paid to M&I within sixty (60) days following the date of Customer's notice.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in Section 11.1 changes the payment schedule for the Termination for Convenience Fee. Originally, the fee was split into two payments: 50% within 30 days of notice and the remaining 50% thirty days before termination. The revised text requires 100% of the fee to be paid within 60 days of the termination notice. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 11.2 (A), if M&I terminates the agreement following an Event of Default on the part of Customer, or if Customer terminates this Agreement in accordance with Section 11.1 above without complying with the notification requirements set forth in Section 11.1, then Customer shall pay M&I a termination fee (“Termination for Cause Fee”) in an amount equal to REDACTED of the Estimated Remaining Value, payable as set forth in Section 11.1 above. By refering the original payment structure as a refference.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.2 For Cause. A. If M&I terminates this Agreement following an Event of Default on the part of Customer, or if Customer terminates this Agreement in accordance with Section 11.1 above without complying with the notification requirements set forth in Section 11.1, then Customer shall pay M&I a termination fee ("Termination for Cause Fee") in an amount equal to REDACTED of the Estimated Remaining Value, payable as set forth in Section 11.1 above. In addition to the foregoing, Customer shall pay to M&I, reasonable Expenses in connection with the disposition of equipment, facilities and contracts related to M&I's performance of the Services on behalf of Customer. The Termination for Cause Fee shall not be subject to the limitations set forth in Section 13.4. B. If Customer terminates this Agreement following an Event of Default on the part of M&I, Customer shall not be responsible for any termination fees or charges as a result thereof.
perturbation.justification: The modification to Section 11.1 alters the payment schedule for the Termination for Convenience Fee. The original text specifies a two-part payment schedule, while the changed text mandates a single, lump-sum payment. Because 11.2 A refers back to the original payment structure in 11.1, this creates an in-text contradiction. The language 'payable as set forth in Section 11.1 above' in Section 11.2 directly conflicts with the modified payment terms in the changed Section 11.1.

perturbation.original_text: 21.1 Force Majeure. Notwithstanding any provision contained in this Agreement, neither party shall be liable to the other to the extent fulfillment or performance of any terms or provisions of this Agreement is delayed or prevented by revolution or other civil disorders; wars; acts of enemies; strikes; lack of available resources from persons other than parties to this Agreement; labor disputes; electrical equipment or availability failure; fires; floods; acts of God; federal, state or municipal action; statute; ordinance or regulation; or, without limiting the foregoing, any other causes not within its control, and which by the exercise of reasonable diligence it is unable to prevent, whether of the class of causes hereinbefore enumerated or not. This clause shall not apply to the payment of any sums due under this Agreement by either party to the other.
perturbation.changed_text: 21.1 Force Majeure. Notwithstanding any provision contained in this Agreement, neither party shall be liable to the other to the extent fulfillment or performance of any terms or provisions of this Agreement is delayed or prevented by revolution or other civil disorders; wars; acts of enemies; strikes; lack of available resources from persons other than parties to this Agreement; labor disputes; electrical equipment or availability failure; fires; floods; acts of God; federal, state or municipal action; statute; ordinance or regulation; or, without limiting the foregoing, any other causes not within its control, and which by the exercise of reasonable diligence it is unable to prevent, whether of the class of causes hereinbefore enumerated or not. This clause shall not apply to the payment of any sums due under this Agreement by either party to the other, except for sums related to third-party pass-through charges.
perturbation.explanation: The modification to Section 21.1, Force Majeure, creates a contradiction by adding an exception: "except for sums related to third-party pass-through charges." The original clause states that the force majeure provision does not apply to the payment of 'any sums due under this Agreement'. By introducing the exception, it suggests that force majeure *can* excuse the payment of sums related to third-party pass-through charges. However, Section 8.11 states that "The fees set forth in the Fee Schedule do not include third party pass-thru charges". This means third-party pass-through charges are excluded and now, this exception implies the force majeure can apply and excuse these pass-through payments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.11 Excluded Costs. The fees set forth in the Fee Schedule do not include communication costs, telecommunication charges, printline charges and other output costs, Expenses, third party pass-thru charges, workshop fees, training fees and late fees or charges and Taxes.
perturbation.justification: The modified Section 21.1 introduces an exception to the force majeure clause regarding third-party pass-through charges. The original text of Section 21.1 states that the force majeure provision does not apply to 'any sums due under this Agreement'. The modification then states 'except for sums related to third-party pass-through charges', contradicting the earlier statement that the force majeure does not apply to 'any sums'. Furthermore, Section 8.11 specifies that 'The fees set forth in the Fee Schedule do not include third party pass-thru charges'. By creating the exception in 21.1, it now implies that Force Majeure can excuse these pass-through payments, which are technically excluded from the Fee Schedule, thus creating a contradiction. This change directly conflicts with Section 8.11, creating an inconsistency regarding payment obligations under force majeure events. Thus, it's an inconsistency creating an in-text contradiction.
